refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, focusing on measurable performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation scenarios; verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure processing latency within teleoperation control systems is minimized.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator with minimal delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement low-latency processing algorithms and hardware acceleration in teleoperation control.
DP3: Employ efficient telemetry data compression and streaming techniques.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to address all latency sources holistically.

potential_sources: Network protocol optimizations (DP1) tightly coupled with telemetry streaming (DP3).
Processing algorithms (DP2) depend on telemetry data format and timing.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are interdependent but designed to minimize cross-domain coupling by modularizing network, processing, and telemetry functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of latency spikes due to unforeseen network congestion or hardware failures.
Assumes telemetry data compression does not degrade critical information.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor latency performance continuously and refine DPs if latency targets are not met in integration testing.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realizes teleoperation control processing, telemetry handling, and network communication optimizations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional communication channel between Teleoperation module and Vehicle module for control commands and telemetry.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Telemetry data stream from Vehicle to Teleoperation for operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Control task commands from NGHE Solution to Teleoperation module.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-related functions; low diffusion as latency logic is centralized.
Vehicle handles telemetry generation but latency control logic is minimal here.
System-level coordination only; latency logic not contained here.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not implement latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control and telemetry processing are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused optimization.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact from Vehicle module telemetry generation delays.
Dependency on network link quality outside Teleoperation control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Coordinate with Vehicle module to optimize telemetry generation timing; monitor network link performance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched by selected DPs addressing all latency sources with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as latency logic is centralized in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for latency control.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency verification is feasible via telemetry and network monitoring tools within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency-related FRs to Teleoperation IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance coordination protocols between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to reduce telemetry delays.
Include network condition monitoring in Teleoperation to adapt latency control dynamically.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment sufficiently addresses the teleoperation latency requirement with clear verification and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure processing latency within teleoperation control systems is minimized.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator with minimal delay.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry data compression does not degrade critical information.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network congestion or hardware failures.
Telemetry generation delays in Vehicle module.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data from integrated teleoperation tests.
